Cumulative Returns vs Benchmark ⓘ

Cumulative returns (equity curve) showing growth of $1 invested; supports comparison between strategy and benchmark.

End of Year Returns vs Benchmark ⓘ

End-of-year returns per calendar year for the strategy and benchmark.

Underwater Plot ⓘ

Underwater plot showing running drawdown (peak-to-trough) over time.

Top Drawdowns for Strategy or Benchmark ⓘ

Top drawdown periods with duration and severity; thresholds above adjust which periods are shown.

Rolling Metrics ⓘ

Rolling metrics plotted over time (Sharpe, Sortino, Volatility, CAGR, Beta, Correlation) for the selected window.

Monthly Returns (%) ⓘ

Monthly returns heatmap showing color-coded monthly returns by year and month.

Returns Distribution ⓘ

Returns distribution histogram/density for the chosen frequency (daily/weekly/monthly).

Historical Allocations ⓘ

Historical allocations over time showing weights for top tickers; use the frequency/source controls to change view.

Key Performance Metrics

Percentage of trading days with an active position.
The CAGR of the risk free rate, used in other risk-adjusted like Sharpe and Sortino.
Total cumulative return over the entire backtest period.
Total cumulative return above the risk free rate over the entire backtest period.
Compound Annual Growth Rate — the annualized rate of return over the backtest period.
Excess Compound Annual Growth Rate — the rate of return minus the risk free rate of return, annualized over the backtest period. Often referred to as "excess returns" when used in other metrics.
Risk-adjusted return measured as excess return per unit of volatility.
Return over Maximum Drawdown — CAGR divided by maximum drawdown magnitude.
Probability that the Sharpe ratio is greater than zero, accounting for skew and kurtosis.
A robust variant of the Sharpe ratio that adjusts for autocorrelation.
Excess return per unit of downside deviation. (Focuses on harmful volatility)
A variant of the Sortino ratio with autocorrelation adjustment.
Sortino ratio normalized by √2 for comparability with Sharpe.
Normalized Smart Sortino for comparability.
Ratio of probability-weighted excess gains to losses across return thresholds.
A volatility indicator which quantifies both depth and duration of drawdown risk.
An alternative risk-adjusted return metric. Similar to Sharpe, but uses the Ulcer index as a measurement of risk instead of volatility.
Largest peak-to-trough decline.
Duration of the maximum loss drawdown period in trading days.
Duration of the longest duration drawdown period in trading days.
Mean magnitude of drawdown periods.
Average duration (market days) of drawdown periods.
Annualized standard deviation of returns. (Risk measure)
Pearson correlation between strategy and benchmark daily returns.
Coefficient of determination — proportion of variance explained by the benchmark.
Excess return over benchmark per unit of tracking error.
CAGR divided by maximum drawdown, a performance-to-risk metric.
Asymmetry of the return distribution; positive means more upside outliers.
Measures tail heaviness of the return distribution. Numbers closer to 3 are more normally distributed.
Average expected daily return.
Average expected monthly return.
Average expected annual return.
Estimated worst expected daily loss with 95% confidence.
Average loss in the worst 5% of days (Conditional VaR).
Ratio of cumulative return to maximum drawdown.
Month-to-date return.
Return over the last 3 months.
Return over the last 6 months.
Year-to-date returns.
Return over the last 1 year.
Annualized return over the last 3 years.
Annualized return over the last 5 years.
Annualized return over the last 10 years.
Annualized return over the entire backtest period (same as CAGR).
Best single-day return.
Worst single-day return.
Best single-month return.
Worst single-month return.
Best single-year return.
Worst single-year return.
Average return on months which have positive returns.
Average return on months which have negative returns.
Ratio of the average win day's excess returns to the average loss day's excess returns.
Ratio of the days with positive excess returns to the days with negative excess returns. Similar to Win Days%, but uses excess returns instead of absolute returns.
Number of trading days with positive returns.
Number of trading days with negative returns.
Percentage of non-zero days with positive returns. Similar to Win Ratio, but uses absolute returns instead of excess returns.
Percentage of non-zero months with positive returns.
Percentage of non-zero quarters with positive returns.
Percentage of non-zero years with positive returns.
